element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns wide variety of punch types through early opponent matches
Spatial dodging in two directions to avoid incoming punches
Accumulate four knockdowns to win by KO within three-round match
Three knockdowns in single round triggers TKO victory condition
Victory determined by punch accuracy, knockdowns, and damage accumulation
Portrait becomes progressively battered and bloody as boxer takes damage
Quest to unite three title belts across different circuits

typical_tension_triggers: Approaching round end without winning condition met
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Three-round match structure with escalating pressure; tension builds through knockdown accumulation; relief occurs upon meeting victory conditions (KO, TKO, or decision)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory opponents in career mode establish basic combat patterns
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased opponent aggression and defensive AI complexity
difficulty_curve.late_game: Quest to unite three fictional boxing circuit title belts with progressively difficult opponents
notable_spikes: Limited character movement within ring criticized as restricting player defensive options
Overwhelming difficulty noted by reviewers as primary gameplay barrier

notable_level_patterns.description: Career mode structured around uniting three separate fictional boxing circuit title belts; progression through series of opponent matches toward championship unification goal
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Career mode matches across three circuits

notable_level_patterns.description: Each bout consists of exactly three rounds; victory conditions achievable through knockout (4 knockdowns), technical knockout (3 knockdowns in single round), or judge decision

sources_used: wikipedia - George Foreman's KO Boxing article (Gameplay section)
